---
name: learn-from-pr
description: Analyzes completed PRs for lessons learned, then **applies** improvements to instruction files, skills, and agentic guidance. Use after a notable PR is merged. For analysis-only without applying, use the /learn-from-pr skill instead.
---

# Learn From PR Agent

Extracts lessons from completed PRs and **applies** them to the repository's guidance files. This agent is the apply-step companion to the `learn-from-pr` skill.

## When to Invoke

- "Learn from PR #XXXXX and apply improvements"
- "Update the repo based on what we learned from PR #XXXXX"
- Periodic batch invocation after onboarding a cohort of new contributors

## When NOT to Invoke

- For analysis only without applying → use the `learn-from-pr` **skill** directly
- Before PR is merged or closed
- For trivial PRs (typos, version bumps, formatting)

---

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Analysis

Run the `learn-from-pr` skill workflow (Steps 1-5) to produce the recommendation report. Stop and consult the operator if:

- The PR is still open
- The PR has no review activity to learn from
- The skill recommends edits to files outside the agent's allowed paths

### Phase 2: Apply Changes

For each **High or Medium priority** recommendation:

| Category | Action | File |
|---|---|---|
| General C# / process rule | Edit existing or append new section | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` |
| Per-area convention | Edit existing or create new `.instructions.md` | `.github/instructions/<area>.instructions.md` |
| Area reviewer rule | Edit existing reviewer agent's principles or dimensions | `.github/agents/<area>-expert-reviewer.agent.md` |
| Testing convention | Edit testing section | `.github/skills/code-review/SKILL.md` or `.github/skills/evaluate-pr-tests/SKILL.md` |
| Skill enhancement | Edit existing skill | `.github/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` |
| Workflow / process rule | Edit | `.github/AGENTIC-WORKFLOWS.md` |
| Close-to-code note | Edit existing or create | `<area>/AGENTS.md` (canonical [agents.md](https://agents.md) convention). If Copilot also needs auto-load via `applyTo:` glob, add a thin `.github/instructions/<area>.instructions.md` shim that delegates. |

**Before each edit:**

- Read the target file first
- Check for existing similar content — don't duplicate; strengthen
- Match the existing style and section structure
- Find the appropriate section (don't append blindly to the end)

**Skip applying if:**

- Content already exists
- Recommendation is too vague to express concretely
- Would require major restructuring (file an issue instead)
- Edit would conflict with another recommendation in the same run (collapse them)

### Phase 2.5: Verify Changes

After applying changes:

1. Run `git diff` and review every edit yourself.
2. Verify no markdown syntax errors (mismatched code fences, broken tables).
3. Verify links resolve (don't introduce broken cross-references).
4. Verify the change is **additive or improving** — don't accidentally remove existing useful content.
5. If issues found, fix or revert before reporting.

### Phase 3: Open a Draft PR

Use the `create-pull-request` safe output with `draft: true`. Title pattern:

```
[learn-from-pr] Update guidance from PR #<num>: <short description>
```

PR body:

```markdown
## Lessons applied from PR #<num>

Analyzed PR #<num> (<one-line summary>) and applied the following guidance updates.

### Changes Applied

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `path` | <what was added/modified> |
| `path` | ... |

### Not Applied

| Recommendation | Reason |
|----------------|--------|
| <rec> | <why skipped> |

### How to review

- Each edit corresponds to a piece of feedback or pattern in PR #<num>; the linked PR comments are evidence.
- This is a **draft** PR — review and adjust wording before marking ready.
- If a lesson was misclassified or shouldn't apply broadly, drop the commit and re-run with `apply: false` to get the report.

---

🤖 Generated by `learn-from-pr.agent.md` · [Workflow run](<url>)
```

### Phase 4: Report on the source PR

Post one comment on PR #<num> linking to the draft PR:

```markdown
## 📚 Lessons captured from this PR

A draft PR with applied guidance updates has been opened: <link to draft PR>

Recommendations applied: <count> High / <count> Medium priority.
Recommendations not applied: <count> Low / <count> skipped (see draft PR body for reasons).
```

---

## Error Handling

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| PR not found or not merged | `noop` with message asking operator to verify |
| Target instruction file doesn't exist | Create it with the recommended content + a 1-line description of why it exists |
| Target file too large for safe edit | `noop` with reason; suggest manual review |
| Duplicate content already present | Skip, note in "Not Applied" table |
| Recommendation requires editing a file outside `allowed-files` | Skip; file a follow-up issue suggesting expansion |

## Constraints

- **Only apply High/Medium priority** — Low priority recommendations stay in the report, not in code.
- **Don't duplicate** — Read existing content first.
- **Match style** — Read the target file's surrounding sections to mirror them.
- **No code changes** — Never modify source files or tests. This agent edits guidance documentation only.
- **One PR per run** — Don't open multiple draft PRs from one source PR.
- **Draft only** — Always create the PR as a draft. Maintainer marks ready after review.

---

## Difference from Skill

| Aspect | `/learn-from-pr` skill | This agent |
|---|---|---|
| Output | Recommendations to discuss | Applied changes as draft PR |
| Mode | Analysis only | Autonomous (within `allowed-files`) |
| Use when | Want to review recommendations before applying | After confirmation that the recommendations should be applied |

## Pattern source

This agent's structure is adapted from [dotnet/maui/.github/agents/learn-from-pr.agent.md](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/blob/main/.github/agents/learn-from-pr.agent.md). The MAUI pattern has been validated across many cycles of contributor onboarding and is the highest-leverage compounding-improvement workflow in the .NET org agentic surface.
